meta.name: Heretics 40K
notes: Single source of truth for all game content and rules. The engine (heretics-40k engine HTML) renders this file.

Scope v1.2: Orbit is now a real named location on every planet (tier 'orbit', holds the ships that used to live on planet.orbit); every Sector gains a Space layer (sector.space). Travel Threads, fleets, and orbital actions attach to these. Location types 'orbit' and 'space' added.
Doors are GRANTED BY location type, not stored per location. Conditions take them away. Access is allegiance-gated.
Filename stays heretics-40k-data-v1.json across the v1.x series; meta.version carries the real version. Git carries the history.
Scores / production / resources are specced here but require persistence (Stage 2) to run. Static demo reads them as constants.

done: more placed NPCs with overview sheets (v1.3)

next.task: Full weapons: Tag Registry + Standard Weapons + Named/Legendary from Tags/Basic Weapons/Named Weapons pages
Full items + abilities from Basic Items / Named & Legendary Items / Abilities pages
Full casts (8 families) from Warp Casting page
Minted planets, populated map, territory ownership per Galaxy & Territory v1

growth.force_xp: Forces earn and rank together; requirement = average of members; joining only at Force's current rank

combat.ap_cost: model AP + equipment AP modifier (additive); Effortless = model AP only
combat.desperation_action: When Force AP pool is empty: 1 action per model per post, limited to Movement or Effortless-tagged use

death.body_state_rule: element of the final point of damage decides the body's state; DoT ticks count

succession.heir: AP-holding Named leader, per declared Succession Order

death.field_recovery: victor recovers 50% of their own revivable dead

revival_window.note: Death is a race, not a flag. The killing element sets the WINDOW; the body must reach an Apothecarion within it.
revival_window.rule: On death the body enters a revival window measured in posts. It must physically reach a location with an Apothecarion door before the window expires. Travel costs count against the window.
revival_window.expiry: Window expires -> permanent death. No door, no cost, no appeal.
revival_window.commanders: Commanders use the same windows. Succession still fires if the window expires.
revival_window.cost: Apothecarion revival costs Currency, scaled to the model's PC.
revival_window.supersedes: revivable_elements / denied_elements are now window lengths, not a binary. Field recovery (50%) unchanged for Physical/Energy.

economy.dominance: Commander's total AP; dispatchable to Named models, granting the Leader tag; a Force without AP takes no actions
economy.influence: ownership ceiling: total PC of all controlled models may never exceed Influence, even split across Forces

thread_types.desc: Spawned by movement; arrival procedures resolve here

rules.comms: In-character remote messaging (vox/astropathic) for NPCs/PCs NOT on the same planet/sector. All face-to-face interaction, including Diplomacy, happens in Threads.

naming_rite.eligibility: Rank 5 unnamed model under a Commander
grants: automatic Named delta for its class (see rules.deltas.named)
repeat of the faction Origin Perk choice from Character Creation (choose 2)

perk_effects.psy: Psyker tag - Warp Cast slot opens

xenos.name: XENOS
xenos.wallets: per-faction currency; Dominance/Influence names TBD per faction

factions.name: Black Legion
backgrounds: You led a claw when the fortress-world died.
appearances: The Eye seared across the faceplate.

factions.name: Death Guard
backgrounds: The Seventh Bell tolled for you alone.
A plague-hulk's last officer; the crew obeys.
Given a seed to plant among the stars.
appearances: Split plate parted by patient flesh.
Ceramite the color of old sickness.

factions.name: World Eaters
backgrounds: The Nails fell silent once. You hunt that hour.

factions.name: Thousand Sons
backgrounds: Sole survivor of one page too far.
Nine urns that counsel you in dreams.
A relic scattered across the Segmentum.
appearances: Sapphire and gilt, nothing inside but purpose.

factions.name: Emperor's Children
backgrounds: You heard the chord and survived.
Rebuilt by a mad chirurgeon. You approve.
appearances: Pupils blown wide, all felt twice.
A cloak that used to sing.

factions.name: Daemons
backgrounds: Clawed free of a greater shadow.
A ward failed. You remember the binder.
All four thrones. None minds. Yet.
appearances: Flesh deciding its form one moment late.
Brass, rot, silk and fire in one hide.
Too wide, in a wrong place.

factions.name: Adeptus Astartes
backgrounds: The gate held. You carry its banner.
Home is a ship you may never see again.
You crossed the Rubicon. The Chapter watches.

factions.name: Astra Militarum
backgrounds: Every officer above you died in one hour.
Home tried to kill you first.
You read every lost campaign, to repeat none.
appearances: Honors in exact order, none unearned.

factions.name: Adeptus Mechanicus
backgrounds: Raised in a dying forge world's cooling halls.
One lost STC; your life its footnote.

factions.name: Adepta Sororitas
backgrounds: Ten sieges unbroken. You are the eleventh wall.
You returned from the Repentia path.
A saint spoke through you once.
appearances: The lily seared over the heart.
Wax on the pauldrons, relic chains.

factions.name: Adeptus Custodes
backgrounds: You left the Palace. The galaxy disappoints.
You guarded a thing best forgotten.
appearances: Gold plate, a plume like spilled blood.
Lightning and aquilae in every surface.

factions.name: Tyranids
backgrounds: Cut from the Hive Mind; kept the hunger, gained a self.
A decade ahead of the fleet.
appearances: Plates scarred by a dead world's weapon.

factions.name: Orks
factions.tagline: Might makes right, an' you'z da biggest.
backgrounds: A shiny bit from every warzone.
appearances: Glyph plates an' a bayonet-biting jaw.

factions.name: Necrons
factions.tagline: Death is a malfunction. You have the patch.
backgrounds: A dust crownworld; protocols answer you.

factions.name: Aeldari
factions.tagline: A dying people, faster than fate.
backgrounds: A farseer spent your fate like coin.
appearances: A war-mask not entirely a mask.

factions.name: Drukhari
factions.tagline: Pain is a currency. You are rich.
backgrounds: The Wych cults chant your name.
You died once. The bill is due.
appearances: Barbed black, made to be seen last.
A shadowfield over hooks best unstudied.

factions.name: T'au
factions.tagline: The Greater Good has a place for you.
backgrounds: You read the forbidden treatises. Twice.
appearances: Ceremony over a used combat rig.

factions.name: Genestealer Cults
factions.tagline: Four arms to embrace the family.

factions.name: Leagues of Votann
backgrounds: You carry the oldest ledger, unclosed.
You found a stratum worth a war.
appearances: A hold's whole heraldry in exo-plate.

factions.name: Harlequins
backgrounds: You played the role none survive.
You know doors that don't exist.
appearances: Colors a half-step ahead of the eye.
The mask laughs; the paint cries.

galaxy.structure: Segmentum > (Core | Expanse zones) > Sector > Planet > Named Locations. Planets ALWAYS sit within a Sector; Expanse zones contain their own Sectors.

planets.name: Vigilus
planets.desc: War-world of the Nachmund Gauntlet. Everyone is here; no one holds it.

locations.name: Vigilus — High Orbit
ships: The Pale Barge - player vessel, system-capable
locations.desc: High orbit over Vigilus. Ships arrive here first; descent to the surface is a separate move.

locations.name: Sangua Tertius — High Orbit
locations.desc: High orbit over Sangua Tertius. Ships arrive here first; descent to the surface is a separate move.

space.name: The Nachmund Void
space.desc: The void of Vigilus Sector. Fleets translate, hold, and give battle here before ever reaching a world.

planets.name: Nurth
planets.desc: Death Guard staging world under a jaundiced sky.

locations.name: Nurth — High Orbit
ships: The Grave Wake - Death Guard picket
locations.desc: High orbit over Nurth. Ships arrive here first; descent to the surface is a separate move.

space.name: The Pallid Deeps
space.desc: The void of Pallid Reach. Fleets translate, hold, and give battle here before ever reaching a world.

planets.name: Kraith Verge
planets.desc: Sskarith's brood-nest. Something legendary gestates below.

locations.name: Kraith Verge — High Orbit
locations.desc: High orbit over Kraith Verge. Ships arrive here first; descent to the surface is a separate move.

space.name: The Kraith Abyss
space.desc: The void of Kraith Drift. Fleets translate, hold, and give battle here before ever reaching a world.

doors.name: Forge
doors.does: craft and upgrade equipment, tiers I-III

doors.name: Apothecarion
doors.does: revive dead models within the revival window, at cost

doors.name: Relay
doors.does: grants Comms range from this location

doors.name: Warp Gate
doors.does: travel shortcut, skips the travel ladder

doors.name: Throne Room
doors.does: seat of a world's ruler; initiates planet-scale threads and events. On a Crown World, initiates SECTOR-scale events.

location_types.name: Hive
location_types.effect: Two shops: the underhive sells what the spire denies exists.

location_types.name: City
location_types.effect: Civilian mass, open trade.

location_types.name: Tradeport
location_types.effect: Widest stock in the game; the only type priced below baseline. First thing a Blockade strangles.

location_types.name: Village
location_types.effect: Tier I goods only. Muster is worth more here than any shop.

location_types.name: Manufactorum
location_types.effect: Highest output of any type. Volume over sanctity - never produces a masterwork.

location_types.name: Forge Temple
location_types.effect: The only ground-side Tier III forge. Taking a Forge World is the only route to Tier III.

location_types.name: Military Outpost
location_types.effect: Field repairs only. Survives warzones because it expects them.

location_types.name: Fortress
location_types.effect: Fortification ladder rung 2. Issues equipment; does not sell it.

location_types.name: Bulwark
location_types.effect: Fortification ladder rung 3. Anchors a region rather than a hill.

location_types.name: Space Station
location_types.effect: Immune to ground famine. Cut the void lanes and it starves faster than any planet.

location_types.name: Orbital Dock
location_types.effect: Where hulls are cut.

location_types.name: Shrine
location_types.effect: No commerce. Altars reach tier III on Shrine Worlds.

location_types.name: Lair
location_types.effect: Xenos ground. You do not buy from a Tyranid - bio-forge only, no shop.

location_types.name: Crown
location_types.effect: The sector capital. Its Throne Room initiates SECTOR-scale events. Raid it and nobody can call a Crusade.

location_types.name: Ruins
location_types.effect: Salvage only. The terminal rung of the condition ladder.

location_types.name: Warzone
location_types.effect: Contested open ground. Grants no doors, by definition.

location_types.name: Plague Garden
location_types.effect: Nurgle's own forge. Reaches Tier III without Mechanicus rites - the Chaos answer to the Forge Temple.

location_types.name: Tomb Vault
location_types.effect: Dormant. Produces nothing until Conflict >= 40 wakes it, then everything at once.

location_types.name: Webway Portal
location_types.effect: Access without ownership. Aeldari reach doors they never conquered.

location_types.name: Mek Shop
location_types.effect: Shouldn't work. Does. Output scales with Conflict rather than population.

location_types.name: Cult Sanctum
location_types.effect: Hidden. Invisible to other factions until sector Conflict >= 40 reveals it.

location_types.name: Orbit
location_types.effect: Planetary orbit — the arrival zone where every Travel Thread resolves and fleets hold station. Orbital docks and stations sit within it.

location_types.name: Deep Space
location_types.effect: The void between worlds. Fleet actions, warp translation, and inter-planet travel resolve here — the Space layer of a Sector.

conditions.name: Intact
conditions.effect: The site works as its type promises.

conditions.name: Fortified
conditions.effect: Player-built. Holder gains; attacker pays more AP to take it.

conditions.name: Consecrated
conditions.effect: Altar +1 tier. Faith casts cheaper for the consecrating allegiance; Warp casts cost x2 for Chaos.

conditions.name: Cursed
conditions.effect: Altar +2 tiers and Muster gains cultists for Chaos; Imperial models take attrition each post.

conditions.name: Infested
conditions.effect: Shops offline; a Lair emerges. Tyranid bio-forge opens.

conditions.name: Besieged
conditions.effect: Gates shut. The forge serves the wall and nobody else.

conditions.name: Sacked
conditions.effect: Taken and stripped. Recoverable - machines survive what people don't.

conditions.name: Ruined
conditions.effect: Structure lost, not merely emptied. Type degrades to Ruins.

conditions.name: Drifting
conditions.effect: Void-side only. No station-keeping, no docking, no arrival.

planet_types.name: Forge World
planet_types.effect: Forge Temples may exist here - the ONLY route to Tier III equipment.

planet_types.name: Hive World
planet_types.effect: Hive locations allowed. Highest population ceiling; highest consumption.

planet_types.name: Industrial World
planet_types.effect: Manufactorums allowed. Volume production, no masterworks.

planet_types.name: Agri World
planet_types.effect: Feeds the sector: while held, sector Famine threshold drops by 10. Lose it and Famine follows.

planet_types.name: Mining World
planet_types.effect: Resources regenerate +50%. The depletion clock's favourite target.

planet_types.name: Civilized World
planet_types.effect: Baseline. No special effect - the control case.

planet_types.name: Shrine World
planet_types.effect: Altars reach Tier III here. The only non-Forge route to a tier-III door.

planet_types.name: Fortress World
planet_types.effect: All fortification types +1 size rung. Sieges cost attackers double AP.

planet_types.name: Feral World
planet_types.effect: Muster costs -50%. Produces recruits, not goods.

planet_types.name: Feudal World
planet_types.effect: Muster costs -50%; Knight-class models available.

planet_types.name: Death World
planet_types.effect: All models take 1 attrition per post without a Bulwark. Produces survivors.

planet_types.name: War World
planet_types.effect: Warzone locations allowed anywhere. Conflict decays 50% slower here.

planet_types.name: Tomb World
planet_types.effect: Tomb Vaults allowed. Dormant until sector Conflict >= 40, then Necron access opens.

planet_types.name: Daemon World
planet_types.effect: Chaos prod x1.0; all others x0.1. Warp casts -50% cost. Reality is optional.

planet_types.name: Xenos World
planet_types.effect: Lairs allowed. Ruling xenos faction gains +1 Muster tier.

planet_types.name: Exodite World
planet_types.effect: Webway Portals allowed. Aeldari only; Taint -2/post while held.

planet_types.name: Sept World
planet_types.effect: Diplomacy threads here grant double Influence. Conflict -3/post.

planet_types.name: Kin Hold
planet_types.effect: Resources never fall below 20. Votann read resources, not status.

planet_types.name: Frontier World
planet_types.effect: Resource Strike events 3x more likely. The Expanse's reward.

planet_types.name: Dead World
planet_types.effect: Produces nothing, forever. Exterminatus output. The one state that never heals.

population_ranks.note: System reads the number; players read the name. Population = whatever that faction converts into output.

sources.note: Population is whatever the faction converts into output - the source differs, the formula does not.
sources.default: civilian dwelling; grows with Peace and production
sources.necrons: awakened legions; rises with Conflict, not peace

status_readings.note: One underlying state. The label changes with the reader; so does the sign on the effect.

Warring.note: War feeds a forge: tier cap holds. Only stock and price move.

Famine.note: Skilled hands starve first, so the tier ceiling drops even where the forge stands.

Corrupted.note: Zero for the faction that LOST it. The ruling allegiance walks in and finds the crucibles hot.

galaxy.status_resolution: worst_of(planet.status, sector.status) - never multiply. A planet can never be better than its sector, only worse.

scores.note: Sector status is DERIVED from these three axes. Not stored, not set by hand. Requires persistence (Stage 2).

prosperity.source: normalised sum of production across all planets in sector

conflict.source: resolved Invasion/Skirmish/Raid threads; decays with quiet posts

taint.source: Chaos/Xenos victories, Warp Casts, cursed conditions

tick.note: Peace rebuilds slowly; war cools slowly; Taint never stops climbing. Left alone, every sector falls.

thread_scales.note: No new top-level thread types. Canon's five, each with a scale and subtypes.

world_enders.note: Throne-only, not Imperial-only. Requires: hold the Crown World, Throne Room intact, Influence, sector Taint >= 60.

chaos.name: Ruination
chaos.effect: Submersion, not destruction. Chaos keeps 0.8 prod where Imperium keeps none.

tyranids.name: Devourment
tyranids.effect: Consumed to the crust. The fleet gains biomass - the only world-ender that pays.

necrons.name: Silence
necrons.effect: Pristine, empty, still. Resources intact, nothing left to reclaim them with.

aeldari.name: Sundering
aeldari.effect: Webway gate collapsed. The world is cut off, not killed.

drukhari.name: Harvest
drukhari.effect: Souls taken, resources intact. They do not want the land.

tau.name: Annexation
tau.effect: The anti-Exterminatus: the planet converts rather than dies.

gsc.name: Uprising
gsc.effect: The world turns from within. No invasion needed.

votann.name: Reclamation
votann.effect: Stripped to nothing. The Kin keep everything.

orks.effect: Orks cannot invoke a world-ender. They can only Waaagh! - and that is a Crusade.

harlequins.effect: Never hold ground, so never hold a Throne.

travel.warp_gate_waiver: Departing through a Warp Gate waives passage entirely (passage = 0) and skips the travel ladder - portal to portal, free.
travel.passage_formula: passage = tier.base * (force total PC / force_divisor); off-planet tiers require a vessel; Warp Gate waives it.

arrival_at_controlled_planet.rule: opens a Travel Thread with entry procedures
arrival_at_controlled_planet.friendly: allied colors recognized: land at any location, draw garrison resupply
arrival_at_controlled_planet.hostile: enemy colors: interception by garrison on approach; ruling faction alerted

travel.forces_tag: every Force carries a location tag, updated on enter/exit
travel.location_thread_history: locations keep thread pages of all activity; movable locations (ships, stations) carry their history with them

shop_catalog.d: Phys 2 - Multihit II - DoT I - Consumable
Warp R3 - Multihit II + DoT II

forge_rules.base: any weapon in inventory (weapons travel, knowledge doesn't)
forge_rules.adds: one affinity-legal tag (tier limited by station) + a name

npcs_alpha.name: Vess the Carrion-Broker
npcs_alpha.hook: trades Drukhari forge access for 250 Currency + 2 intact Tyranid bodies

npcs_alpha.name: Herald of the Pallid Court
npcs_alpha.hook: gateway to Death Guard faction dealings

npcs_alpha.name: Brood-Tyrant Sskarith
npcs_alpha.hook: active Skirmish opponent; broods gestating on Kraith Verge

npcs_alpha.name: Magos Kryv
npcs_alpha.hook: trades data for data; gateway to Mechanicus forge-tech

npcs_alpha.name: The Rust-Warden
npcs_alpha.hook: Kin prospector; buys salvage and mineral rights, asks no questions
